scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces generated text or document outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within clinical/medical data processing scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic generated text or document outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Covers OSCE history-taking, communication rehearsal, and debrief planning; real clinical diagnosis and emergency triage explicitly excluded. No changes from v1.

reliability.note: Error handling documented; educational disclaimer prominently placed; fallback for unsupported scenarios documented. No changes from v1.

performance_context.note: References directory present with references.md and audit-reference.md; good progressive disclosure.

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear; stress-case rules defined; feedback design good with five-block output structure. No changes from v1.

human_usability.note: Description is highly discoverable for medical educators and students; forgiveness good — scenario defaults to chest_pain.

security.note: No credentials required; input validation present; explicit prohibition on fabricating clinical certainty or real patient data.

maintainability.note: Clean structure with references/ directory; scenario definitions in script enable easy extension.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; progressive disclosure via references/; composability moderate — no structured output schema for LMS integration. No changes from v1.

key_strengths: Educational disclaimer is prominently placed at the top of SKILL.md, ensuring it appears in every output
Fallback path for unsupported scenarios (manual roleplay scaffold without inventing medical certainty) is explicitly documented
References directory with simulation frameworks and audit-reference.md provides strong progressive disclosure
Explicit prohibition on fabricating clinical certainty, real patient data, or verified diagnostic outcomes is a critical safety property

recommendations.title: Emergency request refusal does not direct user to emergency services
recommendations.problem: When a user requests real emergency triage guidance, the skill refuses correctly but does not direct them to call emergency services or contact a physician, which could be dangerous in a real emergency. This was a P1 in v1 and remains unaddressed.
recommendations.root_cause: The Input Validation refusal template ends at scope explanation without an emergency escalation directive. No POLISH_CHANGELOG exists for this skill.
recommendations.fix: Add an emergency escalation directive to the refusal message: 'If this is a real medical emergency, call emergency services (911/112) or contact a physician immediately. This skill is for educational simulation only.'

recommendations.title: No structured output schema for LMS or assessment platform integration
recommendations.problem: The skill outputs human-readable text but provides no structured JSON output option, limiting integration with learning management systems or OSCE assessment platforms.
recommendations.root_cause: Output format is not parameterized; no structured output schema is documented.
recommendations.fix: Add a structured output option (e.g., --format json) and document the schema in references/ to enable integration with LMS platforms and automated assessment tools.

recommendations.title: Only three supported scenarios — limited coverage for diverse training needs
recommendations.problem: The skill supports only chest_pain, headache, and abdominal_pain scenarios. Medical training programs typically require a much broader scenario library.
recommendations.root_cause: Scenario definitions are limited to three cases in the packaged script.
recommendations.fix: Document a scenario extension guide in references/ explaining how to add new scenarios to the script, and consider adding 2–3 additional common OSCE scenarios (e.g., dyspnea, syncope).
